headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — consentModal title
message: translation.consentModal.title flows into dom._cmTitle.innerHTML without sanitization. If translations are loaded from a remote endpoint, a compromised endpoint can inject arbitrary scripts.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — consentModal description
message: translation.consentModal.description flows into dom._cmDescription.innerHTML without sanitization. Remote translation endpoints become script-equivalent trust.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — consentModal acceptAllBtn
message: translation.consentModal.acceptAllBtn flows into _cmAcceptAllBtn.firstElementChild.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — consentModal acceptNecessaryBtn
message: translation.consentModal.acceptNecessaryBtn flows into _cmAcceptNecessaryBtn innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — consentModal showPreferencesBtn
message: translation.consentModal.showPreferencesBtn flows into _cmShowPreferencesBtn innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — consentModal footer
message: translation.consentModal.footer flows into dom._cmFooterLinksGroup.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal title
message: translation.preferencesModal.title flows into dom._pmTitle.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal service label
message: categories[*].services[*].label (or the service key as fallback) flows into serviceTitle.innerHTML without sanitization. Dynamic service names could inject HTML.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal section title
message: translation.preferencesModal.sections[*].title flows into sTitle.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal serviceCounterLabel
message: translation.preferencesModal.serviceCounterLabel flows into serviceCounter.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal section description
message: translation.preferencesModal.sections[*].description flows into sDesc.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal cookieTable caption
message: sections[*].cookieTable.caption flows into caption.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal cookieTable header
message: sections[*].cookieTable.headers[*] flows into th.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via insertAdjacentHTML sink — preferencesModal cookieTable body cell
message: sections[*].cookieTable.body[*][*] flows into tdInner.insertAdjacentHTML('beforeend', tdValue) without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal acceptAllBtn
message: translation.preferencesModal.acceptAllBtn flows into dom._pmAcceptAllBtn.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal acceptNecessaryBtn
message: translation.preferencesModal.acceptNecessaryBtn flows into dom._pmAcceptNecessaryBtn.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: DOM XSS via innerHTML sink — preferencesModal savePreferencesBtn
message: translation.preferencesModal.savePreferencesBtn flows into dom._pmSavePreferencesBtn.innerHTML without sanitization.

headline: Regex Injection in Cookie Name Lookup
message: In getSingleCookie, the cookie name is concatenated unescaped into a regex pattern. A name containing regex metacharacters (e.g. dots) can match wrong cookies, potentially misreading consent state. Not externally exploitable — name comes from integrator config.

headline: Command Injection in Build Script (argv → execSync)
message: process.argv[2] flows directly into execSync via template literal without validation. A malicious version string can execute arbitrary commands. Maintainer-only script with no privilege boundary crossed, but becomes dangerous if wired into CI where version is derived from contributor-controlled input.

headline: Ineffective HTML-Stripping Regex (a11y label)
message: The regex /<.*>.*<\/.*>/gm used to strip HTML for screen-reader labels is broken: greedy .* over-matches, and self-closing/void tags are not matched. Result goes to .textContent (safe sink), so not an XSS — but screen readers may announce empty or nonsensical labels.

headline: semver@6.3.1 ReDoS (transitive devDependency)
message: semver@6.3.1 (transitive devDependency via @babel/*) is vulnerable to ReDoS (CVE-2022-25883). Build-time only, not shipped to consumers, not exploitable remotely. The lockfile also contains semver@7.7.1 (patched) for other dependents.
